citation_and_locator: Tong, Angel Man-Shan & Kwing Lok Albert Lee. 2024. An acceptability study of triadic constructions in Hong Kong Cantonese. Lingua 307:103749. DOI 10.1016/j.lingua.2024.103749; pp.1-11; examples 1-3; Table 2; Figures 1-2
what_it_supports: Directly distinguishes DOC S-V-IO-DO, PDC S-V-DO-P-IO, and IDOC S-V-DO-IO; gives opposite teach/send lexical contrasts; reports a 559-participant audio acceptability study crossing DO/IO weight, pause, and age.
limit: Target ratings were generally low; the study excludes matrix bei2 GIVE; its weight categories are experimental operationalizations; age is nonmonotonic; production frequency is not measured.

citation_and_locator: Tong & Lee 2024, p.8 raw-mean prose/Fig.1 versus pp.8-10 model interpretation and discussion
what_it_supports: Establishes that pause presence affected ratings and that the intended theoretical contrast concerns prosodic integration.
limit: Raw-mean prose and the figure indicate pause > no-pause, while model/discussion/conclusion state pause lowers acceptability. Direction must be resolved from data/code or author clarification.

citation_and_locator: Xu, Liejiong & Alain Peyraube. 1997. On the Double Object Construction and the Oblique Construction in Cantonese. Studies in Language 21(1):105-127. DOI 10.1075/sl.21.1.05lie; project extraction pp.105-113
what_it_supports: Supports V-theme-recipient, V-recipient-theme, and V-theme-bei2-recipient contrasts; lexical class, Goal/Source role, object weight, focus, and competing preposition-deletion analysis all restrict distribution.
limit: 1990s author judgments and a contested derivational analysis do not establish current frequency, universal category, or a binary weight rule.

citation_and_locator: Tang, Sze-Wing. 1998. On the Inverted Double Object Construction. In Studies in Cantonese Linguistics, pp.35-52; project source-screen and proposition records
what_it_supports: Provides direct comparisons among V-DO-bei2-IO, V-IO-DO, and V-DO-IO, including verb-class, prosodic, speaker-variation, and discourse-completeness judgments.
limit: One theoretical account with author/informant judgments; marked and speaker-variable examples require source-specific preservation.

citation_and_locator: Chin, Andy Chi-on. 2022. Cantonese GIVE and Double-Object Construction: Grammaticalization and Word Order Change. John Benjamins. DOI 10.1075/scld.15; chapters 5-7 and appendices 5-8 listed by publisher
what_it_supports: Confirms a dedicated monograph treats Yue double-object typology, IO-DO/DO-IO relations, ongoing word-order change, and a 40-speaker sociolinguistic survey.
limit: Exact examples, statistics, and conclusions were not inspected in full text in this checkpoint; publisher description cannot carry detailed construction claims.

citation_and_locator: Tong, Angel Man-Shan. 2018. Cantonese Prosodic Syntax and Nuclear Stress Rule. CUHK doctoral dissertation; cited and summarized by Tong & Lee 2024
what_it_supports: Supplies the immediate hypothesis source for verb restrictions, IO weight, focus shift, prosodic rephrasing, and age observations.
limit: Dissertation full text and complete examples were not directly inspected here; claims remain secondary until extracted.
